results.title: Uptime Commitment

results.quoted_text: Northwind shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain 99.9% uptime, measured monthly, excluding scheduled maintenance. Customer's sole and exclusive remedy for failure to meet this commitment is a service credit as set forth in Exhibit A.
results.explanation_simple: The clause commits Northwind to a specific uptime percentage but limits the customer's remedies.
results.why_it_matters: The 99.9% uptime commitment is significant for service reliability, but the limitation to a service credit may not fully compensate the customer for downtime.
results.legal_insight: This clause defines service performance commitments and the consequences if the provider fails to meet them.
results.market_comparison: The uptime commitment is common in service agreements, but the limitation of remedies to a service credit may be seen as less favorable to the customer.
results.red_flag_reason: The limitation of remedies to a service credit may expose the customer to significant operational risk without adequate compensation.
results.recommendation: Check whether uptime targets, measurement periods, exclusions, remedies, and reporting obligations are clear.
results.safer_alternative: In the event of failure to meet the uptime commitment, the Customer shall be entitled to a service credit and the option to terminate the agreement if uptime falls below 99.9% for two consecutive months.

7.1 Customer may not terminate this Agreement for convenience prior to the end of the then-current Subscription Term. Northwind may terminate this Agreement for convenience at any time upon ninety (90) days' written notice, with a pro-rata refund of prepaid, unused fees.
